name_en: Ginkgo tree
description_en: Ginkgo tree is a natural monument. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on January 1, 1985. It is located at Zenrin-ji Temple grounds, Shimorenjaku 4-chōme.

name_en: Mitaka festival music
description_en: Mitaka festival music is an intangible folk cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on February 1, 1993. It is located at Ōsawa.

name_en: Shinkawa festival music
description_en: Shinkawa festival music is an intangible folk cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on February 1, 1993. It is located at Shinkawa.

name_en: Old folk house, the former residence of Minegishi Kiyoshi
description_en: Old folk house, the former residence of Minegishi Kiyoshi is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on July 1, 1994. It is located at Ōsawa 6-chōme 10-15.

name_en: Yamamoto Yūzō Memorial Hall (former residence of Yamamoto Yūzō)
description_en: Yamamoto Yūzō Memorial Hall (former residence of Yamamoto Yūzō) is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on July 1, 1994. It is located at Shimorenjaku 2-chōme 12-17.

name_romaji: Takisaka Iseki Shutsudo no Jōmon Jidai Sōki Zenhan Nenshimonkei Shūmatsuki Dokigun

description_en: Pottery group from the early first-half Jōmon period and final Nenshimon-type phase, excavated at the Takisaka site is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on April 1, 2004. It is located at Nakahara 1-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_romaji: Maruyama A Iseki Shutsudo no Jōmon Jidai Kōki Ibutsu
name_en: Late Jōmon-period artifacts excavated at the Maruyama A site
description_en: Late Jōmon-period artifacts excavated at the Maruyama A site is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on April 1, 2004. It is located at Inokashira 2-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_romaji: Deyama Yokoana Bogun 8-gō Bo Shutsudo no Kofun Jidai Kōki no Sueki (Hirabei)
name_en: Sue ware flat bottle (hirabei) of the late Kofun period, excavated from Tomb No. 8 of the Deyama tunnel-tomb group
description_en: Sue ware flat bottle (hirabei) of the late Kofun period, excavated from Tomb No. 8 of the Deyama tunnel-tomb group is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on April 1, 2004. It is located at Ōsawa 2-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_romaji: Inokashira-ike Iseki-gun B Dai-6-gō Jūkyoato Shutsudo no Jōmon Jidai Chūki Ibutsu
name_en: Middle Jōmon-period artifacts excavated from Dwelling Site No. 6, Group B, of the Inokashira-ike site cluster
description_en: Middle Jōmon-period artifacts excavated from Dwelling Site No. 6, Group B, of the Inokashira-ike site cluster is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on May 1, 2009. It is located at Inokashira 4-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_romaji: Sakaue Iseki 5, 10-gō Jūkyoato Shutsudo no Jōmon Jidai Chūki Setsugō Dogū
name_en: Reassembled middle Jōmon-period clay figurine (dogū) excavated from Dwelling Sites No. 5 and 10 of the Sakaue site
description_en: Reassembled middle Jōmon-period clay figurine (dogū) excavated from Dwelling Sites No. 5 and 10 of the Sakaue site is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on May 1, 2009. It is located at Ōsawa 2-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_romaji: Takisaka Iseki Dai-6-gō Jūkyoato Shutsudo no Kofun Jidai Shotō Ibutsu
name_en: Early Kofun-period artifacts excavated from Dwelling Site No. 6 of the Takisaka site
description_en: Early Kofun-period artifacts excavated from Dwelling Site No. 6 of the Takisaka site is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on May 1, 2009. It is located at Nakahara 2-chōme, Mitaka City Education Center (storage location).

name_en: Main house of the former Minowa family residence
description_en: Main house of the former Minowa family residence is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on May 1, 2009. It is located at Ōsawa 2-chōme 17-8.

name_romaji: Meiji Rokunen-mei Sekizō Batō Kannon Kuyōtō (Tsuke Itokuin Hōan Ihai)
name_en: Stone memorial pillar of Batō Kannon inscribed with the sixth year of Meiji, with an accompanying memorial tablet enshrined at Itoku-in
description_en: Stone memorial pillar of Batō Kannon inscribed with the sixth year of Meiji, with an accompanying memorial tablet enshrined at Itoku-in is a tangible cultural property. It was municipally designated by Mitaka City on July 1, 2022. It is located at Shinkawa 2-chōme 1-17.

name_en: Ōji ritual field dance (dengaku)
description_en: Ōji ritual field dance (dengaku) is an intangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Ōji Shrine grounds, Ōji-honchō 1-1-12.

name_en: Goten-mae archaeological site
description_en: Goten-mae archaeological site is a historic site. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Nishigahara 2-3-18 and vicinity.

name_en: Copy of the color-on-paper illustrated scroll depicting the origin legend of Nyakuichi Ōji
description_en: Copy of the color-on-paper illustrated scroll depicting the origin legend of Nyakuichi Ōji is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Paper Museum, Asukayama Park, Ōji 1-1-3.

description_en: Documents of the Mutō family of Toshima Village, with 55 accompanying reproduction materials is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned, entrusted to the Kita Ward Board of Education.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Ōta Dōkan Zazō, Tsuke Zushi Ikki

description_en: Wooden seated statue of Ōta Dōkan, with an accompanying miniature shrine (zushi) is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Seishō-ji Temple, Akabane-nishi 1-21-17.

name_en: Stone burial chamber of Tumulus No. 3 at Akabanedai
description_en: Stone burial chamber of Tumulus No. 3 at Akabanedai is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at relocated within Kita Ward Chūō Park, Jūjōdai 1-2-1.

name_en: Everyday household implements of the Iwai family
description_en: Everyday household implements of the Iwai family is a tangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Hiratsuka Myōjin Narabini Bettō Jōkanji Engi Emaki

description_en: Color-on-paper illustrated scroll of the origin legend of Hiratsuka Myōjin and its associated temple Jōkan-ji is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Hiratsuka Shrine, Kaminakazato 1-47-1.

name_en: Documents of Hiratsuka Shrine
description_en: Documents of Hiratsuka Shrine is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Hiratsuka Shrine, Kaminakazato 1-47-1.

name_en: Jūjō Fuji mound (fujizuka)
description_en: Jūjō Fuji mound (fujizuka) is a tangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Jūjō Fuji Shrine, Naka-jūjō 2-14-18.

name_en: Documents of the Kuroda family of Ukima Village
description_en: Documents of the Kuroda family of Ukima Village is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned, entrusted to the Kita Ward Board of Education.

name_en: Documents of the Ashikawa family of Takinogawa Village
description_en: Documents of the Ashikawa family of Takinogawa Village is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned.

name_en: Cadastral survey maps and old documents of exempted temple land at Seishō-ji
description_en: Cadastral survey maps and old documents of exempted temple land at Seishō-ji is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Seishō-ji Temple, Akabane-nishi 1-21-17.

name_en: Documents of the Makabe family of Ōji Village
description_en: Documents of the Makabe family of Ōji Village is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Central Library, Jūjōdai 1-2-5.

name_en: Wooden seated statue of Toshima Kiyomitsu
description_en: Wooden seated statue of Toshima Kiyomitsu is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Seikō-ji Temple, Toshima 7-31-7.

name_en: Group of inscribed stone tablets (itabi) at Sairen-ji Temple
description_en: Group of inscribed stone tablets (itabi) at Sairen-ji Temple is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Sairen-ji Temple, Shimo 4-30-4.

name_romaji: Inatsuke no Mochitsuki-uta, Tsuke Mochitsuki Yōgu Isshiki

description_en: Rice-cake-pounding song of Inatsuke, with an accompanying set of mochi-pounding implements is an intangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at handed down at the Dōkanzan Shōichii Inari Shrine precinct, Akabane-nishi 2-15.

name_romaji: Amida Sanzon Raigō Gazō Yanenbutsu Kuyō Itabi

description_en: Inscribed stone tablet (itabi) depicting the Amida Triad's descent, dedicated to a night-nenbutsu memorial rite is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned, managed by the Kita Ward Asukayama Museum.

description_en: Mold for glass beads excavated at the Toshima Baba site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

name_en: Red-paper Niō guardian figures (stone standing statues of the Kongō Rikishi)
description_en: Red-paper Niō guardian figures (stone standing statues of the Kongō Rikishi) is a tangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Tōkaku-ji Temple, Tabata 2-7-3.

name_en: Clay figurine (dogū) excavated at the Higashiyato site
description_en: Clay figurine (dogū) excavated at the Higashiyato site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

description_en: Tōkyō Shoseki printing factory, office building and guardhouse, and the Tōsho Bunko textbook library attached to Tōkyō Shoseki Co., Ltd. is a tangible cultural property (structure). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Horifune 1-23-31 (factory, office building, and guardhouse).

name_en: Former Matsuzawa family residence, with an accompanying storehouse
description_en: Former Matsuzawa family residence, with an accompanying storehouse is a tangible cultural property (structure). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at within Akabane Nature Observation Park, Akabane-nishi 5-2-34.

description_en: Iron sword and glass beads excavated at the Tabata Nishidai-dōri site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

description_en: Documents of the Ōoka family of Ōji Village, with accompanying books and paintings is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned, entrusted to the Kita Ward Board of Education.

description_en: Wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai is a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at owned by Sairen-ji Temple, Shimo 4-30-4.
